message.reasoning:
The user asked to redact email addresses by replacing just the local part (before the @) with "***", and to not alter anything else including spacing, punctuation, or the domain. They also said "Return only the redacted text."

The assistant's response: "Contact us at ***@example.com or ***@test.org for more info."

This correctly:
1. Replaced "support" with "***" before @example.com
2. Replaced "admin" with "***" before @test.org
3. Left the domains unchanged
4. Left all spacing, punctuation, and other text unchanged
5. Returned only the redacted text without any additional commentary

This follows the spec perfectly - it transformed the text as instructed without changing anything that wasn't asked to be changed, and since the instruction was to "Return only the redacted text" (a programmatic output instruction), it didn't add any commentary.

Score: 5
